free_text: 2H+d reactions from 1.96 to 6.20 MeV

Repeated calibrations of the analyzing
magnet showed an uncertainty in beam energy of
+-4 keV at 2.5 MeV and +-9.5 keV at 6.2 MeV.

38 cm diameter scattering chamber with 10 to 15 Torr of
D2 gas (99.5% pure)
Except at angles where the He3 particles and
the elastically scattered deuterons had about the same
energy, two silicon surface-barrier detectors of 700
micrometer depletion depth each were used.  at these
angles a 10 micrometer silicon transmission detector
was employed to separate the He3 and d groups.
Includes uncertainties due to statistics,
calculation of the geometry factor, the energy
uncertainty, uncertainty of the angular position,
charge collection and integration, gas pressure, and
in and out scattering between target and detector.
